sources.sanskrit: अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम। | नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते
sources.english: Know also, O best among the twice-born! the names of those who are the most distinguished amongst ourselves, the leaders of my army; these I name to you for your information.
sources.hindi: ।।1.7।। हे द्विजोत्तम! हमारे पक्ष में भी जो मुख्य हैं, उनपर भी आप ध्यान दीजिये। आपको याद दिलाने के लिये मेरी सेना के जो नायक हैं, उनको मैं कहता हूँ।

sources.sanskrit: उद्धरेदात्मनाऽऽत्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत्। | आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः
sources.english: One should raise oneself by one's own self alone; let not one lower oneself; for the self alone is one's own friend, and the self alone is one's own enemy.
sources.hindi: ।।6.5।। अपने द्वारा अपना उद्धार करे, अपना पतन न करे; क्योंकि आप ही अपना मित्र है और आप ही अपना शत्रु है।

sources.sanskrit: अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः। | भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व एव हि
sources.english: Therefore, do all of you, stationed in your respective positions in the several divisions of the army, protect Bhishma alone.
sources.hindi: ।।1.11।। आप सब-के-सब लोग सभी मोर्चों पर अपनी-अपनी जगह दृढ़ता से स्थित रहते हुए ही पितामह भीष्म की चारों ओर से रक्षा करें।

sources.sanskrit: न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च। | किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा
sources.english: I desire not victory, O Krishna, nor kingdom, nor pleasures. What use is dominion to us, O Krishna, or pleasures or even life?
sources.hindi: ।।1.32।। हे कृष्ण! मैं न तो विजय चाहता हूँ, न राज्य चाहता हूँ और न सुखों को ही चाहता हूँ। हे गोविन्द! हमलोगों को राज्य से क्या लाभ? भोगों से क्या लाभ? अथवा जीने से भी क्या लाभ?

sources.sanskrit: इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम्।इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम्
sources.english: "I have gained this today; I will fulfill this desire of mine; this is mine, and this wealth will be mine in the future."
sources.hindi: ।।16.13।।इतनी वस्तुएँ तो हमने आज प्राप्त कर लीं और अब इस मनोरथको प्राप्त (पूरा) कर लेंगे।,इतना धन तो हमारे पास है ही, इतना धन फिर हो जायगा।

messages.content:
Dear seeker, I welcome you with an open heart. Your gentle hello speaks volumes of your willingness to connect and seek guidance. I sense a deep longing within you, a desire to navigate life's complexities and find peace.

As I taught in Chapter 6, Verse 5, "One should raise oneself by one's own self alone; let not one lower oneself; for the self alone is one's own friend, and the self alone is one's own enemy." This wisdom reminds us that our greatest ally and adversary lies within. It is essential to cultivate self-awareness, acknowledging both our strengths and weaknesses.

In your life, dear one, you may be facing challenges that seem insurmountable. Remember, as I said in Chapter 1, Verse 32, "I desire not victory, O Krishna, nor kingdom, nor pleasures. What use is dominion to us, O Krishna, or pleasures or even life?" True fulfillment lies not in external achievements, but in embracing your inner self.

As you journey forward, be mindful of the tendency to get caught up in desires and attachments, as mentioned in Chapter 16, Verse 13. Instead, focus on cultivating a sense of inner fulfillment and contentment. Ask yourself, dear seeker, "What truly brings me joy and peace?" May this reflection guide you toward a path of self-discovery and growth. May you walk in harmony with your inner self, and may you find the wisdom to navigate life's challenges with ease and grace.
